column2: Fields mainly used for crops that are sown and harvested at least once a year (e.g. cereals, vegetables, legumes, oilseeds).

2.1 Land uses and production systems pre: Perennial / tree crops (orchards, plantations of tree crops, perennial horticulture)
column2: Areas dominated by long-lived crops that remain in place for several years (e.g. coffee, cocoa, fruit orchards, olives, vineyards, tea).
Areas mainly used for grazing animals on natural or sown grasses and herbaceous vegetation (e.g. pastures, rangelands, transhumant grazing areas).
Landscapes where crops and livestock are managed together in an integrated way (e.g. crop fields with regular grazing, manure cycling between fields and animals).
Areas where trees are deliberately integrated with crops and/or livestock as part of the production system (e.g. shade-grown coffee, alley cropping, trees in pastures).
Tree plantations primarily managed for wood, pulp, or fibre production (e.g. eucalyptus, pine, acacia timber plantations).

2.1 Land uses and production systems pre: Rice paddies and other flooded cropland
column2: Cropland that depends on managed flooding or standing water for part of the production cycle (e.g. irrigated rice systems).
Artificial or managed waterbodies used mainly for fish, crustacean, or aquatic organism production.

2.1 Land uses and production systems pre: E. Natural and semi-natural areas within the landscape
Natural or semi-natural forest, shrubland or woodland
column2: Forest, woodland or shrubland patches that are not primarily managed as plantations and retain natural or semi-natural vegetation.

2.1 Land uses and production systems pre: Natural or semi-natural grassland, savanna or shrub-steppe
column2: Open vegetation areas that are not primarily managed as sown pastures but retain natural or semi-natural characteristics.

2.1 Land uses and production systems pre: Wetlands and natural waterbodies (lakes, rivers, marshes, riparian zones)
column2: Natural or semi-natural aquatic and wetland features within or adjacent to the monitored area (e.g. rivers, streams, ponds, marshes, riparian strips).

2.1 Land uses and production systems pre: Other land use or production system (please specify)
column2: Any other type of land use present that does not fit the categories above.
